title: dianthus repens subsp. schistosus
intro: Dianthus repens subsp. schistosus, also known as the 'Schistous creeping pink', is a subspecies of the Dianthus repens plant, a type of flowering plant that belongs to the carnation family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues and as a diuretic, due to its rich content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The plant has been found to offer health benefits such as reducing inflammation, relieving pain, and exhibiting antimicrobial properties. Herbal preparations of Dianthus repens subsp. schistosus, typically in the form of infusions or tinctures, are often used to support digestive health, reduce fever, and promote urinary tract health.
